intro:
There is not enough information available on Tragacantha cruentiflora, however, I found some information on Tragacanth (also known as Tragacanth gum, Tragacantha or Dragantis). 

Tragacanth is a type of plant-based gelling agent derived from the sap of the Astragalus plant, particularly the Tragacanth gum exuding from the stems and branches of certain Astragalus species. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, due to its protective and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tragacanth is commonly used in traditional medicine and herbal preparations, including ointments, salves, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
